message:
fix(copilot_cli,opencode): handle nested data.content + prefer DB probe paths

copilot_cli: Chronicle-format session events (since gh-copilot 0.0.342)
nest message payloads under a `data` object:
  {"type":"user.message","data":{"content":"..."}}
Prior to this change, extract_content only inspected top-level fields, so
every Chronicle events.jsonl produced zero conversations. Now extract_content,
extract_event_message (fallback branch), extract_timestamp, and session_id /
workspace discovery all descend one level into `data` / `payload` / `metadata`
envelopes. Legacy top-level schemas still win when present. Two regression
tests added.

opencode: default probe roots now list `.local/share/opencode/opencode.db`
(and `.config/opencode/opencode.db`) before their parent directories so
diagnostic tooling surfaces the actual data file instead of the sibling
config dir (cass issue #188). Removed the lexicographic sort in detect_roots
that was shadowing `.local/share/opencode` behind `.config/opencode`.

utils: renamed duplicate `mod tests` block so `cargo test --lib` compiles.

Closes cass#187 (parser) and unblocks cass#188 (probe ordering).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
